output: Completed batch 4 as a no-op assessment. The T6 structure is sufficient: `resizeOverrides` stays local to `TimelineCanvas`, `SortableRow` is memoized, and each row receives only its own resize-override subset, so only the actively resized row should rerender during preview. No ref-plus-forceUpdate path was needed. task_updates.executor_notes: Assessed the resize-preview rerender path after T6 and kept it as a no-op. `resizeOverrides` remains local to `TimelineCanvas`, the memoized `SortableRow` receives only row-scoped override subsets, and the row render path uses a shared empty override object for unaffected rows; combined with `TimelineEditor` owning `getActionRender` outside that local resize state, this is sufficient for only the actively resized row to rerender during preview. No ref+forceUpdate path was needed. Verified by code inspection in `TimelineCanvas.tsx` lines 284, 321-339, and 739-747 plus `TimelineEditor.tsx` lines 329-390, and by the full video-editor Vitest module passing (23 files / 82 tests).
